title: eragrostis vulgaris subsp. major
intro: Eragrostis vulgaris subsp. major, also known as the weeping lovegrass, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, relieving fever, and soothing digestive issues. In traditional medicine, weeping lovegrass is prepared as an infusion or tincture, and is sometimes used in combination with other herbs to create teas and tonics.
